workflow.name: Create daily prayer and sunnah fasting reminders in Google Calendar using Aladhan API
workflow.description:
🕌 How it works

This workflow automatically creates daily Islamic reminders in your Google Calendar based on your location 📍

It fetches real-time prayer times 🌐 and generates events for:
	•	🕌 Daily prayers (Subuh, Dzuhur, Ashar, Maghrib, Isya)
	•	🤲 Morning & evening dhikr (Doa Pagi & Petang)
	•	🌙 Sunnah fasting reminders (Ayamul Bidh – 13, 14, 15 Hijri)

To prevent duplicates 🔍, it compares generated events with existing calendar entries 📅 and only creates new ones if they do not already exist ✅

⸻

⚙️ Setup steps
	•	Set your location (city & country) in the Set City and Country node
	•	Connect your Google Calendar account
	•	Adjust the schedule trigger if needed (default: daily)
	•	Activate the workflow

Setup takes less than 5 minutes ⏱️

⸻

⚠️ Notes
	•	This workflow uses the Aladhan API for prayer times and Hijri date conversion
	•	Timezone is set to Asia/Jakarta (you can adjust it if needed)
	•	Detailed logic and explanations are available inside the workflow using sticky notes

⸻

💡 Tip

Perfect for building consistent daily worship habits using automation 🤖

workflow.name: Automatically Add Daily Prayer & Sunnah Fasting Reminders to Google Calendar (n8n)

parameters.content:
## Schedule Trigger & Location Setup
- This workflow runs daily and allows you to configure your city and country.

- Modify the "Set City and Country" node to match your location.
- Default: Bandung, Indonesia
- Change this to your own location

## Fetch Prayer Times from API
This section retrieves daily prayer times based on the configured location.

## Reformat Prayer
Cleans and formats prayer time data into a simpler structure for further processing.

## Get Date (Hijri API)

Fetches today's Hijri date using Aladhan API.

## Hijri Date & Sunnah Logic

Checks if today is 13, 14, or 15 Hijri.
If true, adds a sunnah fasting reminder event.

## Generate Events
- Creates a list of events with title, start time, and end time.
- Each item represents one calendar event.

## Check Existing Events
- Matches new events with existing ones using title comparison.
- Used to detect duplicates

## IF Event Do Not Exist

- Checks if the event already exists in the calendar.
- If not found → proceed to create event.
- If found → skip.

## Create an Event
Creates events only if they do not already exist.

Dont forget to Set up your google calendar account

## Get Today's Existing Events

- Retrieve all events scheduled for today (00:00–23:59).
- Used to prevent duplicate events from being created.


Dont forget to Set up your google calendar account

🕌 Smart Prayer & Sunnah Reminder (Google Calendar + Hijri + n8n)

This workflow automatically creates daily Islamic reminders in your Google Calendar:
	•	🕌 Daily prayer times (Subuh, Dzuhur, Ashar, Maghrib, Isya)
	•	🌅 Morning & evening dhikr (Doa Pagi & Petang)
	•	🌙 Sunnah fasting reminder (Ayamul Bidh – 13, 14, 15 Hijri)

Prayer times are dynamically fetched based on your location, and Hijri dates are used to determine sunnah fasting days.

⸻

🔁 Flow

Schedule Trigger
→ Set City & Country
→ Get Prayer Times (API)
→ Format Data
→ Get Hijri Date
→ Detect Ayamul Bidh
→ Generate Events
→ Get Today’s Events (Google Calendar)
→ Compare (Anti-Duplicate)
→ Create Event (if not exists)

⸻

⚙️ Setup Guide

1️⃣ Configure Location

Open node: Set City and Country

Replace:
city: Bandung
country: Indonesia

With your own location.

⸻

2️⃣ Google Calendar Credential
	1.	Add Google Calendar OAuth2 in n8n
	2.	Connect your Google account
	3.	Select your calendar in the “Create Event” node

⸻

3️⃣ Set Schedule

Open: Schedule Trigger

Recommended:
Run once daily (e.g., 00:05 AM)

⸻

4️⃣ Activate Workflow
	•	Save workflow
	•	Click “Activate”
	•	Done 🎉

⸻

⚠️ Important Notes

🔁 Anti-Duplicate Protection

This workflow:
	•	Checks existing events for today
	•	Compares events by title
	•	Only creates new events if they do not already exist

⸻

🕒 Timezone

Make sure your n8n timezone is set to:
Asia/Jakarta

⸻

📅 Event Format

Events are created using:
	•	Summary (title)
	•	Start time
	•	End time

⸻

💡 Customization Ideas

You can extend this workflow by:
	•	Sending reminders via Telegram / WhatsApp
	•	Using latitude & longitude instead of city
	•	Supporting multiple users
	•	Adding notification reminders
	•	Integrating AI for Islamic content

⸻

🏆 Use Case

Perfect for:
	•	Daily ibadah reminders
	•	Building consistent habits
	•	Learning n8n automation with real use case

⸻

🚀 Tips

If events are not created:
	•	Check Google Calendar credentials
	•	Ensure workflow is active
	•	Verify city & country input

Building scalable solutions using Python, SQL, n8n, and modern data tools.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
